title: CBP Reveals CAPE System Progress: Automated IEEPA Refund Engine Under Construction
desc: A new court filing by CBP Executive Director Brandon Lord reveals that the CAPE (Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries) system is actively under development within ACE. The four-component system — Claim Portal (70% complete), Mass Processing (40%), Review & Liquidation (80%), and Refund (60%) — will automate the end-to-end IEEPA tariff refund process for importers.
source: Court Filing — Atmus Filtration v. United States

title: CIT Accepts CBP's Seven-Step IEEPA Tariff Refund Process
desc: The Court of International Trade approved CBP's proposed seven-step automated refund process. Importers will file declarations in ACE, which will validate entries and recalculate duties without IEEPA tariffs.

title: Liberty Justice Center sues to block Section 122 tariffs
desc: The Liberty Justice Center filed a lawsuit challenging Trump's Section 122 tariffs, arguing the statute does not authorize broad-based tariffs. The suit follows the Supreme Court's ruling striking down IEEPA tariffs as unconstitutional.

title: IEEPA Tariff Refunds: CIT Orders & Next Steps for Importers
desc: Cherry Bekaert outlines the practical next steps for importers seeking IEEPA tariff refunds, including preserving records, checking liquidation timelines, and setting up ACE accounts for electronic refund processing.

title: CBP Outlines IEEPA Tariff Refund Process, Could Be Ready in 45 Days
desc: Orrick details CBP's proposed automated refund process through its ACE system, which would let importers file declarations and receive electronically processed refunds. Less than 10% of eligible importers have set up accounts so far.

title: Who Gets the Tariff Refund? Retailers and Downstream Buyers May Have Claims
desc: Jones Walker examines whether retailers and downstream buyers who absorbed IEEPA tariff costs through higher prices can recover their share of refunds from the importer of record.

title: CBP tells judge it can't comply with tariff refund order
desc: U.S. Customs and Border Protection told the Court of International Trade it cannot immediately comply with the order to refund $166 billion in IEEPA tariffs, citing technological limitations. CBP proposed a new automated system that could be ready in 45 days.

title: Customs officials to lay out tariff refund plans to US trade judge
desc: The Hill reports on CBP's proposal to build a streamlined refund process for IEEPA tariffs, which would save over 4 million man-hours compared to manual processing. The CIT suspended its immediate compliance order pending the new system.

title: CIT Suspends Earlier Order Directing IEEPA Tariff Refunds
desc: Judge Eaton paused the immediate refund order after CBP filed a declaration explaining its systems cannot process refunds at the required scale. The court is now considering CBP's proposed 45-day timeline for an automated refund system.

title: NY Attorney General leads lawsuit to stop Section 122 tariffs
desc: New York Attorney General Letitia James leads a 24-state coalition suing to block Trump's Section 122 replacement tariffs, arguing they lack the statutory basis required and that no qualifying balance-of-payments deficit exists.

title: Judge orders U.S. Customs to process refunds on illegal Trump tariffs
desc: The Court of International Trade ordered CBP to begin processing refunds of IEEPA tariffs for all importers of record, not just those who filed lawsuits. CBP says the task is unprecedented, requiring review of over 70 million entries.

title: Trade court orders Trump administration to kickstart tariff refund process
desc: Axios reports on the CIT's March 4 order directing CBP to liquidate and reliquidate all entries without regard to IEEPA tariffs, benefiting all 300,000+ importers who paid duties under the struck-down tariff regime.

title: States led by New York sue to block Trump's Section 122 tariffs
desc: A coalition of 24 states filed suit in the Court of International Trade challenging the replacement Section 122 tariffs, arguing they violate the statute's nondiscrimination requirement and lack the required factual findings for product exemptions.

title: What Every Multinational Should Know About the CIT's Order to Refund All IEEPA Tariffs
desc: Foley & Lardner analyzes the CIT's broad refund order, explaining the universal scope of relief, the distinction between liquidated and unliquidated entries, and practical steps multinational importers should take now.

title: Companies are entitled to refunds for Trump tariffs struck down by Supreme Court, judge rules
desc: CBS News reports that Judge Eaton's ruling extends refund eligibility to all importers who paid IEEPA tariffs, not just those who challenged them in court. The administration is expected to appeal.

title: Judge rules companies are entitled to refunds for Trump tariffs overturned by the Supreme Court
desc: NBC News reports on the CIT order in Atmus Filtration v. United States directing CBP to refund all illegally collected IEEPA tariffs. More than $130 billion in tariff payments are at stake.

title: CIT Orders CBP to Refund IEEPA Tariffs Following Supreme Court Ruling
desc: ArentFox Schiff analyzes the CIT's order directing CBP to reliquidate entries and refund IEEPA tariffs, noting the broad scope covering all importers and the expected appeal by the Trump administration.

title: Judge orders US to stop calculating IEEPA tariffs for importers
desc: Bloomberg reports Judge Eaton ordered CBP to halt all liquidation of entries involving IEEPA tariffs and begin the process of unwinding duties already assessed, in a sweeping order covering all importers of record.

title: Bessent says global 15% tariff starts this week, plans to move back to prior rates
desc: Treasury Secretary Bessent announced the Section 122 tariff rate will increase to 15% and signaled the administration plans to restore prior IEEPA-level tariff rates within 5 months through new legislative authority from Congress.

title: Supreme Court IEEPA decision reshapes trade authority and refund opportunity
desc: PwC analyzes how the Supreme Court's ruling fundamentally reshapes presidential trade authority and outlines the refund opportunity for importers, including eligibility for tariffs paid since February 4, 2025.

title: IEEPA Tariff Refund: Key FAQs for Importers
desc: BDO addresses frequently asked questions about IEEPA tariff refunds, including eligibility criteria, filing procedures for Post-Summary Corrections and protests, and timelines for the refund process.
